104	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
105	
106	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
107	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
108	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
109	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
110	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
111	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
112	
113	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
114	
115	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
116	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
117	
118	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
119	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
120	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
121	
122	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
123	```
124	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
125	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
126	```
127	folded with the `IM = M` clause. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the body is a
128	typed `sorry`. The signature is non-tautological: it asserts a function
129	`(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks 00LF definition. -/
130	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
131	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
132	  sorry
133	
134	end Module
135	
136	end RingTheory
137	
138	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
139	
140	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
141	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
142	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
143	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
144	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
145	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
146	
147	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
148	
149	namespace Module
150	
151	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
152	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
153	
154	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
155	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
156	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
157	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
158	
159	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
160	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
161	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
162	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
163	
164	iter-176+: body is `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)`.
165	For the iter-175 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry` — the wrapper is
166	genuinely a one-liner once the universe / abbreviation choices are settled,
167	but we leave it as a typed `sorry` to keep the file-skeleton uniform. -/
168	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
169	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
170	  sorry
171	
172	end Module
173	
174	namespace RingTheory
175	
176	namespace Module

178	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
179	
180	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
181	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
182	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
183	
184	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
185	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
186	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
187	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
188	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
189	
190	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
191	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
192	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
193	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
194	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
195	
196	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/
197	
198	/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
199	with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
200	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
201	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
202	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.
203	
204	iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
205	of `Ext^*(κ, -)` applied to `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` for a non-zero-divisor
206	`x ∈ 𝔪`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(κ, M) ≠ 0 ↔ depth(M) = 0`, which is
207	the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
208	`𝔪 ∈ Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC). For the iter-175 file-skeleton
209	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
210	theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
211	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
212	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
213	    [Nontrivial M] (n : ℕ) :
214	    (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ↔
215	      ∀ i : ℕ, i < n →
216	        ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
217	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
218	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 := by
219	  sorry
220	
221	/-! ## §4. Depth on a short exact sequence
222	
223	For a short exact sequence `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` of nonzero finite modules
224	over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
225	crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
226	long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation
227	of §3.
228	
229	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` (Stacks tag 00LE). -/
230	
231	/-- **Depth on a short exact sequence.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
232	ring and let
233	```
234	0 → N' → N → N'' → 0
235	```
236	be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite `R`-modules (encoded by an
237	injection `f : N' →ₗ[R] N`, a surjection `g : N →ₗ[R] N''`, and
238	`Function.Exact f g`). Then
239	1. `depth(N) ≥ min(depth(N'), depth(N''))`,
240	2. `depth(N'') ≥ min(depth(N), depth(N') - 1)`,
241	3. `depth(N') ≥ min(depth(N), depth(N'') + 1)`.
242	
243	The three inequalities are read off from the long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)`
244	sequence applied to the short exact sequence and the depth-via-Ext
245	characterisation (§3 above).
246	
247	iter-176+: the body invokes `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on each module
248	and chases the long exact sequence. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the
249	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
250	theorem depth_of_short_exact
251	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
252	    {N' N N'' : Type u}
253	    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
254	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
255	    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
256	    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
257	    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
258	    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
259	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
260	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
261	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
262	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
263	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
264	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
265	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
266	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
267	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
268	  sorry
269	
270	end Module
271	
272	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
273	
274	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
275	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
276	```
277	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
278	```
279	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
280	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
281	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
282	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
283	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
284	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
285	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
286	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
287	
288	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
289	
290	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
291	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
292	dimension. Then
293	```
294	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
295	```
296	
297	The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
298	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
299	finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ℕ∞`-arithmetic
300	subtleties).
301	
302	iter-176+: the body is the induction on `depth(M)` outlined in the
303	chapter — base case via the minimal-finite-free-resolution +
304	"what is exact" + iterated `Module.depth_of_short_exact`; inductive step
305	via the snake lemma on multiplication by a common non-zero-divisor and
306	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`. For the iter-175
307	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
308	theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
309	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
310	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
311	    [Nontrivial M]
312	    (n : ℕ)
313	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
314	    (n : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
315	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
316	  sorry
317	
318	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
319	
320	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
321	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
322	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
323	
324	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
325	
326	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
327	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
328	
329	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
330	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
331	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
332	this is the upstream gap-fill.
333	
334	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
335	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
336	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
337	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
338	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
339	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
340	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
341	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
342	  depth_eq_krullDim :
343	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
344	
345	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
346	
347	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
348	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
349	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
350	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
351	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
352	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
353	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
354	
355	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
356	
357	namespace CohenMacaulay
358	
359	/-- **Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay.** Every regular Noetherian
360	local ring is Cohen–Macaulay: a minimal generating set of `𝔪` is an
361	`R`-regular sequence of length `dim R`, so `depth(R) ≥ dim R`; combined
362	with the standard upper bound `depth(R) ≤ dim R` (Stacks 00LK) this gives
363	`depth(R) = dim R`.
364	
365	This is the consumer-facing input for **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
366	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective
367	surface): the local ring `O_{S,x}` of a regular projective surface at a
368	closed point is regular of Krull dimension `2`, hence Cohen–Macaulay,
369	hence has depth `2`, which is exactly the input the local-cohomology
370	vanishing `H^i_x(O_S) = 0` for `i < 2` needs (Stacks 0AVF; see Hartshorne
371	III.7).
372	
373	iter-176+: the body is either the direct regular-sequence argument
374	outlined in the chapter, or via Auslander–Buchsbaum applied to all finite
375	`R`-modules (any finite `R`-module has `pd_R(M) ≤ d`; specialising to
376	suitable `M` gives the equation). For the iter-175 file-skeleton the body
377	is a typed `sorry`. -/
378	instance of_regular (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
379	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] : CohenMacaulay R where
380	  depth_eq_krullDim := by

# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)

This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).

## Status (iter-176 Lane I file-skeleton)

This file is the **iter-176 Lane I** file-skeleton (re-dispatch of the iter-175
Lane I that died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without ever
calling `Write`). Each of the five blueprint-pinned declarations carries the
*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`) with a `sorry`
body. The bodies are iter-177+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` (A.2.b)
landing their pinned declarations.

The 5 pinned declarations are:

1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
   representing quotient `Q`.
4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.

## Note on type expressivity

Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:

- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
  tautological `Spec k`.
- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
  `picSharp C |_T`.
- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
  group axioms.

The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders
for the sibling-chapter functors `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` and `Div_{C/k}`) are
typed-`sorry` *carriers* used to express the 5 pinned declarations' types
without forward-referencing into Lean files that have not yet landed. They
ship in the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` namespace, marked with
docstrings explaining they will be re-exports of the sibling-chapter
landing once `Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` land
(forward refs `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp` and
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor` per their blueprint `\lean{...}`
pins). Their bodies collapse to a one-line `:= ...` re-export at that
point.

## References

Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` (640 LOC,
5 pins). Source: Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 9 §9.4;
arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 25–35), specifically:
- §3 Def. `dfn:Abel` (the Abel map) + Thm. `th:repDiv` (relative effective
  divisors representable by an open subscheme of the Hilbert scheme),
- §4 Main Thm. `th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch` (FGA representability),
- §4 Lem. `lm:qt` (smooth-proper quotient of étale sheaves).
-/

154	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
155	
156	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
157	`C/k` is the `k`-scheme representing the étale-sheafified relative Picard

193	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
194	
195	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
196	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` (an `S`-flat
197	closed subscheme whose ideal is invertible) determines a `T`-point of the
198	Hilbert functor `Hilb_{C/k}(T)` (hence of `Div_{C/k}(T)`), and the dual
199	ideal sheaf `O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` is an invertible sheaf on the
200	relative curve, representing a class in `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)`.
201	
202	The assignment `D ↦ [O_{C ×_k T}(D)]` is functorial in `T` (pullback of an
203	ideal sheaf along `id_C ×_k g : C ×_k T' ⟶ C ×_k T` takes the ideal of `D`
204	to the ideal of `D_{T'}`, since `D` is flat over `T` and hence
205	`D_{T'} = D ×_T T'` is again a relative effective divisor by Kleiman §3
206	Lem. `lm:ctn`).
207	
208	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
209	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
210	
211	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`, sending a
212	relative effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated
213	invertible sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
214	
215	A natural transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`. The source
216	`divFunctor C` is the relative-divisor functor (the open subfunctor of the
217	Hilbert/Quot scheme cut out by the effective-divisor criterion); the target
218	`picSharp C` is the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor.
219	
220	iter-177+: the body constructs the natural transformation component-wise:
221	for each test `T`, send a relative effective Cartier divisor `D` on
222	`C ×_k T` to the dual of its ideal sheaf, then take the étale-sheafified
223	class. Naturality is the functoriality of the dual-of-ideal-sheaf
224	construction under pullback (Stacks 01HG + Kleiman §3 Lem. `lm:ctn`).
225	For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
226	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
227	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
228	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
229	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
230	    divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C :=
231	  sorry
232	
233	/-! ## §3. The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent
234	
235	The structural lemma underlying Step 4 of Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` is the
236	Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma**: given a surjection
237	`α : Z ⟶ P` of étale sheaves with `R := Z ×_P Z` representable, `Z`
238	quasi-projective, and the first projection `R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper,
239	the quotient `P` is representable by a quasi-projective scheme.
240	
241	Kleiman's proof:
242	1. `R ↪ Z ×_k Z` is a flat (from (iv)) and proper monomorphism, hence a
243	   closed immersion by EGA IV 8.11.5.
244	2. By the theory of flat-and-proper effective equivalence relations on
245	   quasi-projective schemes (Altman–Kleiman), there exist a
246	   quasi-projective scheme `Q` and a faithfully flat, projective map
247	   `Z ⟶ Q` with `R = Z ×_Q Z`. The map `Z ⟶ Q` is smooth (its fibers
248	   coincide with those of `R ⟶ Z`, which are smooth).
249	3. In the category of étale sheaves, both `Q` and `P` are coequalisers of
250	   `R ⇒ Z`; uniqueness of the coequaliser gives `Q ≃ P`.
251	
252	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_proper_quotient` (Kleiman §4 Lem.
253	`lm:qt`). -/
254	
255	/-- **The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent of an
256	étale-sheaf surjection.**
257	
258	Let `Z, P : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u` be presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`, and let
259	`α : Z ⟶ P` be a natural transformation. Assume:
260	1. `Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `Y` (encoded by the
261	   `Z.RepresentableBy Y` hypothesis),
262	2. the pullback presheaf `R := Z ×_P Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `R`
263	   (encoded by the `(Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R` hypothesis),
264	3. the first projection `π : R ⟶ Y` (after representation) is smooth and
265	   proper (encoded by the typeclass hypotheses `[Smooth π.left]` and
266	   `[IsProper π.left]`),
267	4. `α` is an étale surjection (encoded as the lift property: every
268	   `T`-point `p ∈ P(T)` lifts to a `T'`-point `z ∈ Z(T')` for some test
269	   morphism `T ⟶ T'`).
270	
271	Then `P` is representable (by a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Q`).
272	
273	iter-177+: the body builds `Q` via the Altman–Kleiman effective quotient
274	of the flat-and-proper equivalence relation `R ↪ Y ×_k Y`, then exhibits
275	the coequaliser-uniqueness in the category of étale sheaves to identify
276	`Q` with the representing object of `P`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton
277	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
278	theorem smoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
279	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
280	    (α : Z ⟶ P)
281	    (Y : Over (Spec (.of k)))
282	    (_hZ : Z.RepresentableBy Y)
283	    (R : Over (Spec (.of k)))
284	    (_hR : (Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R)
285	    (π : R ⟶ Y)
286	    [Smooth π.left] [IsProper π.left]
287	    (_hα : ∀ ⦃T : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ⦄ (p : P.obj T),
288	        ∃ (T' : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ) (e : T ⟶ T') (z : Z.obj T'),
289	          α.app T' z = P.map e p) :
290	    P.IsRepresentable := by
291	  sorry
292	
293	/-! ## §4. The FGA representability theorem
294	
295	Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Picard scheme (Kleiman §4 Thm.
296	`th:main`), specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k`,
297	`X = C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve: the étale-sheafified
298	relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` is representable by a `k`-scheme,
299	separated and locally of finite type, which is a disjoint union of open
300	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes. The representing scheme is the **Picard
301	scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of `PicScheme` above.
302	
303	Blueprint reference: `thm:fga_pic_representability` (Kleiman §4 Thm.
304	`th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch`). -/
305	
306	/-- **FGA representability of the Picard scheme.**
307	
308	Let `k` be a field and `C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
309	over `k`. Then the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`
310	is representable by the Picard scheme `PicScheme C`.
311	
312	iter-177+: the body assembles the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof:
313	1. Stratify `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` by Hilbert polynomial into a disjoint open
314	   cover `(P^φ)_φ`.
315	2. For each `φ`, bound twists by `m`-regularity to reduce to `P^{φ_0}_0`.
316	3. Factor through the Abel map `A_{C/k}` of `abelMap` to obtain
317	   `Z := P^{φ_0}_0 ×_{Pic^♯} Div_{C/k}`, a quasi-projective open of
318	   `Div_{C/k}`.
319	4. Apply `smoothProperQuotient` to the projection `Z ⟶ P^{φ_0}_0` to
320	   represent each `P^{φ_0}_0`; reassemble over `φ` for the full
321	   `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
322	
323	For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
324	noncomputable def representable {k : Type u} [Field k]
325	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
326	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
327	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
328	    (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C) :=
329	  sorry
330	
331	/-! ## §5. The abelian group-scheme structure
332	
333	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` inherits an abelian group-scheme structure
334	from the tensor product of invertible sheaves on `C ×_k T`. The relative
335	Picard presheaf `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` is already an abelian
336	group at every `T` (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
337	functorially in `T`; this lifts to the étale sheafification (sheafification
338	of an abelian-presheaf is an abelian-sheaf, since it commutes with finite
339	products in the value category), and then to a group-scheme structure on
340	the representing scheme by Yoneda.
341	
342	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_is_group_scheme` (Kleiman §2 Def. `df:Pfs` +
343	§4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
344	
345	/-- **The Picard scheme is a `k`-group scheme.**
346	
347	The Picard scheme `PicScheme C` carries a canonical `GrpObj` structure (a
348	group-object structure in `Over (Spec (.of k))`), obtained by Yoneda
349	transport of the abelian-group structure on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)` (sum:
350	`[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`; inverse: `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`; unit: `[O_{C ×_k T}]`).
351	
352	The full abelian-group-scheme refinement is the conjunction of this `GrpObj`
353	instance with the `IsCommMonObj (PicScheme C)` predicate (commutativity of
354	the multiplication), which holds because tensor product of invertible
355	sheaves is symmetric; `IsCommMonObj` is a separate instance threaded on top
356	of `GrpObj` in iter-177+.
357	
358	iter-177+: the body invokes the Yoneda lemma to transport the natural
359	abelian-group structure on `T ↦ picSharp C |_T` to the multiplication /
360	inverse / unit morphisms on `PicScheme C`; the group axioms hold at the
361	representable level functorially in `T`, hence on the representing scheme
362	by Yoneda. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
363	noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
364	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
365	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
366	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
367	    GrpObj (PicScheme C) :=
368	  sorry
369	
370	end PicScheme
371	
372	end Scheme
373	
374	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/

68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g

81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1

92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf

132	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
133	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
134	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
135	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
136	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
137	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
138	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
139	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))

142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
175	
176	/-- Genus-`0` Albanese witness. The witness object is the trivial terminal scheme
177	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec k))`; the `JacobianWitness` structural fields (proper, smooth,
178	geometrically irreducible, genus-stable, group-object) reduce to the
179	corresponding trivialities on `Spec k` via the structural cluster
180	`(𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)`. The `isAlbaneseFor` field consumes the **genus-0
181	abelian-variety rigidity** statement: for every pointed `(C, P)` and every
182	target group scheme `(A, η[A])`, the genus-0 hypothesis forces a morphism
183	`f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]` to equal `toUnit C ≫ η[A]`. Over an
184	algebraically closed base `k̄` this is
185	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (in
186	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, char-free, route-C, iter-163).
187	For a general `[Field k]` base the keystone is consumed via pullback to
188	`Spec k̄` + descent of the morphism equality along the faithfully-flat
189	surjection `Spec k̄ → Spec k`. The uniqueness clause cancels the epimorphism
190	`toUnit C` via `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective` (smooth ⟹ flat,
191	geometrically irreducible ⟹ surjective on `Spec k`).
192	
193	**Status (iter-172):** the `key` body remains `sorry`; the rigidity consumer
194	exists (declared) but still propagates upstream sorries from the iter-171
195	body skeleton in `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`. Wiring closes once
196	(i) the `Genus0BaseObjects` body-skeleton internal sorries discharge, and
197	(ii) the `k → k̄` pullback / descent step is filled. -/
198	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
200	    (h : genus C = 0) :
201	    JacobianWitness C where
202	  J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
203	  grpObj := inferInstance
204	  -- `(𝟙_).hom` is defeq `𝟙 (Spec k)`, but instance search does not unfold it;
205	  -- supply each instance for the identity and let term-elaboration close by defeq.
206	  proper := (inferInstance : IsProper (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
207	  smooth := (inferInstance : Smooth (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
208	  geomIrred := geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k
209	  smoothGenus := by
210	    rw [h]; exact (inferInstance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
211	  isAlbaneseFor := by
212	    -- For every `k`-rational marked point `P`, exhibit the Albanese datum for
213	    -- the pointed curve `(C, P)` with `J = 𝟙_` (blueprint C.2 / C.3).
214	    intro P
215	    -- The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map `toUnit C`.
216	    refine ⟨toUnit C, ?_, ?_⟩
217	    · -- Pointed condition: both `P ≫ toUnit C` and `η[𝟙_]` are maps `𝟙_ ⟶ 𝟙_`,
218	      -- equal by uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object.
219	      exact toUnit_unique _ _
220	    · -- Universal factorisation: given `f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]`, the
221	      -- factoring morphism `𝟙_ ⟶ A` is the identity element `η[A]`.
222	      intro A _ _ _ _ f _hf
223	      -- Genus-0 RIGIDITY (C.2): `f` equals the constant morphism at the identity.
224	      -- The route-C content `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (declared in
225	      -- `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`) is char-free and import-clean
226	      -- (proven iter-163 onward). To consume it for the general `[Field k]` goal,
227	      -- this body needs to pull back `(C, A, f, P)` along an algebraic closure
228	      -- `Spec k̄ → Spec k`, apply `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` in the
229	      -- pulled-back setting, then descend the morphism equality along the
230	      -- faithfully-flat surjection (the descent step itself is
231	      -- `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`, already used below to cancel the
232	      -- terminal epimorphism). The pullback functor + the descent of a morphism
233	      -- equality is the residual sub-build; it is real but is not gated on
234	      -- any out-of-file plan-level decision.
235	      have key : f = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
236	        sorry
237	      refine ⟨η[A], key, ?_⟩
238	      -- Uniqueness: any `g'` with `f = toUnit C ≫ g'` equals `η[A]`, by cancelling
239	      -- the epimorphism `toUnit C`. Its underlying scheme morphism is `C.hom`
240	      -- (`Over.toUnit_left`), which is faithfully flat (smooth ⟹ flat) and
241	      -- surjective (a geometrically irreducible curve over a field is nonempty and
242	      -- `Spec k` is a single point), hence an epimorphism (`Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`).
243	      intro g' hg'
244	      have hcancel : toUnit C ≫ g' = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by rw [← hg', ← key]
245	      have hepiL : Epi (toUnit C).left := by
246	        rw [Over.toUnit_left]; exact Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective C.hom
247	      haveI : Epi (toUnit C) := Over.epi_of_epi_left _
248	      exact (cancel_epi (toUnit C)).mp hcancel

276	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
277	curve. This is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C Jacobian
278	scaffolding (the other being `genusZeroWitness`); both are scheduled for body
279	closure post M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. It packages all five protected sorries
280	(`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
281	`instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records
282	that the Albanese property of the underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every
283	choice of $k$-rational marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor`
284	field of `JacobianWitness`).
285	
286	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
287	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
288	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
289	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
290	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
291	existence is assumed.

294	genus C = 0` decomposition consuming `genusZeroWitness` (genus-0 arm)

301	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
302	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := by
303	  by_cases h : genus C = 0
304	  · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩
305	  · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩

311	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :

317

300	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group

316	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]

318	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
319	    [IsProper C.hom]
320	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
321	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
322	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
323	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
324	    (f : C ⟶ A)
325	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
326	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
327	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
328	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
329	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus
330	  set g : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A := φ.inv ≫ f with hgdef
331	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
332	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
333	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
334	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
335	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
336	    exact _hf
337	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
338	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
339	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
340	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
341	    exact hpoint
342	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
343	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
344	  calc f
345	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
346	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
347	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
348	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]
349	    _ = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
350	        rw [← Category.assoc,
351	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]
352	
353	
354	end AlgebraicGeometry

import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects
import AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma

/-!
# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly

This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).

The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.

The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:

1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.

The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.

See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).

## Encoding notes

Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus

/-!
# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)

This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.

The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).

## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)

This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.

The 6 pinned declarations are:

1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
   Body gated on iter-178+ `SymmetricPower.lean` substrate (Milne III.3
   Proposition 3.1; Mumford 1970 II.7 / III.11 affine-and-glue recipe).
3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap` (noncomputable def, ~5 LOC) —
   the symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` produced by
   `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` from an input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian` (noncomputable def,
   ~5 LOC) — the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
   `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`.
5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma` (theorem,
   ~10 LOC) — the descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism
   `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` via Milne Theorem 3.2.
6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
   the headline UP theorem (Milne Proposition III.6.1).

## File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`

The A.3 row of Route A (the identity-component refinement of the Picard
scheme together with its degree map) has not yet been split out into a
dedicated chapter or `.lean` file at the time of this file-skeleton
landing. We supply a **single helper** here: a `Pic0.Bundle` structure
bundling the underlying scheme together with its four abelian-variety
instances (`GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `Smooth`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`),
plus a typed-`sorry` carrier `Pic0.bundle C` and a derived definition
`Pic0.jacobianScheme C := (bundle C).scheme`. The four instances on
`jacobianScheme C` are derived from the bundle's fields (no extra
`sorry`). Once the A.3 chapter materialises this placeholder collapses to
a one-line re-export.

Helper budget = 1: the single helper carrying a `sorry` is `Pic0.bundle`.

## Note on type expressivity

Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:

- `abelJacobi C P₀ : C ⟶ jacobianScheme C` — a genuine morphism into the
  Pic⁰ scheme (not `𝟙 C` or any tautology).
- `SymmetricPower C g : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` — a `k̄`-scheme, not `C`
  itself or `𝟙_`.
- `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A` — a morphism into
  the abelian variety `A`.
- `symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C`
  — a morphism into Pic⁰.
- `descentThroughBirationalSigma` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying
  `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g ≫ ψ`.
- `albanese_universal_property` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ`.

Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
(a morphism into the Pic⁰ scheme, a unique factorisation through the
Abel–Jacobi map, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.

## Abelian-variety conventions

Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:

- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).

A **smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:

- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.

These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site.

## References

Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex` (~830 LOC,
6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §III.6 Proposition 6.1, p. 104;
§III.3 Proposition 3.1 (symmetric power), p. 94; §III.5 Theorem 5.1(a)
(birationality of `f^{(g)}`), p. 101 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`).
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Pic0

/-! ## §0. File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`

The A.3 row of Route A (identity component of the Picard scheme together
with its degree map) is not yet split out into a dedicated chapter / file.
The single helper below — a bundled structure carrying the Pic⁰ scheme
together with its four abelian-variety instances, plus a typed-`sorry`
carrier `bundle` — supplies the placeholder. Once the A.3 chapter
materialises this collapses to a one-line re-export.

This is the **only** helper in the file that consumes a `sorry`. The
derived `jacobianScheme` def and the four typeclass instances below are
projections from `bundle` and do not introduce additional `sorry`s. -/

/-- File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`: bundles the underlying
`k̄`-scheme together with the four abelian-variety structural instances
(group object, proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible) supplied at the
A.3 row of Route A.

Collapses to a re-export of the A.3 chapter once that materialises. -/
structure Bundle {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
  /-- The underlying scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
  scheme : Over (Spec (.of kbar))
  /-- Group-object structure on `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
  grpObj : GrpObj scheme
  /-- Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
  proper : IsProper scheme.hom
  /-- Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
  smooth : Smooth scheme.hom
  /-- Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible scheme.hom

variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]

variable (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]

/-- File-internal **placeholder carrier** for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — a typed
`sorry` pending the A.3 row chapter. iter-178+: replace with the
`Bundle` produced by `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean` (A.2.c) after
its identity-component / degree-map refinement (A.3) lands. -/
noncomputable def bundle : Bundle C := sorry

/-- The underlying `k̄`-scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of a smooth proper
geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed field
`k̄`. Read off from the `Bundle` placeholder. -/
noncomputable def jacobianScheme : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (bundle C).scheme

/-- Group-object structure on `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (jacobianScheme C) := (bundle C).grpObj

/-- Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
instance instIsProper : IsProper (jacobianScheme C).hom := (bundle C).proper

/-- Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
instance instSmooth : Smooth (jacobianScheme C).hom := (bundle C).smooth

/-- Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
instance instGeomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible (jacobianScheme C).hom :=
  (bundle C).geomIrred

/-! ## §1. The Abel–Jacobi morphism at a marked point

The first sub-lemma packages the construction and elementary regularity
of the Abel–Jacobi morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at the basepoint
`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint
itself `ι_{P₀}(P₀) = η_J`.

This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a
representable group scheme); the morphism is the moduli classifier of
the rigidified diagonal correspondence
`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.

Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/

/-- **The Abel–Jacobi morphism at the marked basepoint `P₀`.**

For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an
algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked `k̄`-point
`P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the **Abel–Jacobi morphism** is the unique regular
morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` characterised on `T`-points by the
formula `(1 × ι_{P₀})^* 𝓜^{P₀} ≈ 𝓛^{P₀}`, where `𝓜^{P₀}` is the
Poincaré–Picard correspondence on `C × Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` and
`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})`.

iter-178+: body constructed by applying the moduli interpretation of
`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`def:pic_scheme` together with the identity-component
refinement A.3) to the relative degree-zero line bundle `𝓛^{P₀}` on
`C × C → C` along the second projection. Yoneda then produces the
unique classifying morphism `C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The basepoint
condition `P₀ ≫ abelJacobi C P₀ = η[Pic⁰_{C/k̄}]` (`lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`)
holds because restriction of `𝓛^{P₀}` to `C × {P₀}` is the trivial
line bundle `𝓞_C(P₀ − P₀) ≅ 𝓞_C` whose moduli class is `η_J`.

For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
noncomputable def abelJacobi (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) :
    C ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
  sorry

/-! ## §2. The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`

The `g`-th symmetric power of `C` is the quotient of the `g`-fold
Cartesian power `C^g` by the natural permutation action of the
symmetric group `S_g`. Mathlib `b80f227` has no formalised symmetric
power of a scheme — only `Sym` / `SymmetricPower` for types and
modules — so the scheme-theoretic construction must be supplied
project-side, following Milne's recipe of §III.3 Proposition 3.1: on
each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C` form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then glue
along the standard open-cover patching (Mumford 1970 §II.7 / §III.11).

This sub-build is a non-trivial dependency of A.4.d that the prover
must absorb before the body of `symmetricPowerAVMap` /
`symmetricPowerToJacobian` closes; the carrier here records the
substantive *target* `Sym^g C : Over (Spec k̄)` with a typed `sorry`
body, deferring the affine-and-glue construction to iter-178+
`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean`.

Blueprint reference: `def:symmetric_power_curve` (Milne, *Abelian
Varieties*, §III.3, Proposition 3.1, p. 94). -/

/-- **The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`** of a complete nonsingular
curve `C` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, as a `k̄`-scheme.

Milne III.3 Proposition 3.1 constructs `Sym^g C` as the quotient of
`C^g` by the natural permutation action of the symmetric group `S_g`:
on each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C`, form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then
glue along the standard open-cover patching. The construction comes
equipped with a canonical finite surjective symmetric morphism
`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` characterised by the universal property that
every `S_g`-symmetric morphism `C^g ⟶ T` factors uniquely through `π`.

This pin records the substantive *target* scheme `Sym^g C`; the
projection `π` and its universal property are recorded as helper API
in the iter-178+ `Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate file.

iter-178+: body is the gluing-of-affines `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`
construction over an open affine cover of `C`. For the iter-177
file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
noncomputable def SymmetricPower (_g : ℕ) : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
  sorry

/-! ## §3. The morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`

The second sub-lemma is the elementary fact that the symmetric
assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` from `C^g` to `A`
descends through `π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C`. The key point is that the
`g`-fold addition morphism `add_A : A^g ⟶ A` is itself `S_g`-symmetric
because the underlying group object `A` is commutative as a group
scheme; combined with the universal property of `Sym^g C`, this yields
the unique factorisation.

Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` (Milne's proof of
III.6.1, p. 104, "Clearly this is symmetric, and so it factors through
`C^{(g)}`"). -/

/-- **The symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`.**

Let `φ : C ⟶ A` be a morphism into an abelian variety `A` over `k̄`.
The composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g} : C^g ⟶ A^g ⟶ A` sending
`(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is `S_g`-equivariant (where `S_g`
permutes the factors of `C^g` and acts trivially on `A`), because
addition in a commutative group object is order-independent. By the
universal property of `Sym^g C` (`def:symmetric_power_curve`) it
factors uniquely as `Sym^g φ ∘ π = add_A ∘ φ^{×g}`.

iter-178+: body is the universal-property invocation on the
symmetric composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g}` once the iter-178+
`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate exposes the projection `π`
and its UP. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
noncomputable def symmetricPowerAVMap (g : ℕ)
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (_φ : C ⟶ A) :
    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A :=
  sorry

/-! ## §4. The birational morphism `Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`

The third sub-lemma is the birational identification of `Sym^g C` with
`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. After choosing the basepoint `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the cycle
map `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ [𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)]` defines a regular
morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` which is birational by
Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) (the proof uses dimension count plus the
Riemann–Roch identification of the generic fibre as a single point on
a curve of genus `g`).

Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian` (Milne,
*Abelian Varieties*, Theorem III.5.1(a), p. 101). -/

/-- **The birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`.**

Given a basepoint `P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the symmetrisation of the Abel–Jacobi
morphism `add_J ∘ ι_{P₀}^{×g} : C^g ⟶ J^g ⟶ J` descends through
`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` to a regular morphism
`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` sending an effective divisor class
`[P₁ + ⋯ + P_g]` to `[𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`.
By Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) this morphism is *birational*: it is
dominant with one-dimensional fibres, and its generic fibre is a single
`k̄`-point (the linear system `|D|` of a general degree-`g` divisor `D`
on a curve of genus `g` is `0`-dimensional by Riemann–Roch).

The birationality data (a dense open subset `U ⊆ Sym^g C` mapping
isomorphically onto a dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) is recorded as
helper API in the iter-178+ proof body of
`descentThroughBirationalSigma`.

iter-178+: body is the specialisation of `symmetricPowerAVMap` to
`A := jacobianScheme C` and `φ := abelJacobi C P₀`. For the iter-177
file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
noncomputable def symmetricPowerToJacobian
    (_P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) (g : ℕ) :
    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
  sorry

/-! ## §5. Descent through the birational quotient

The final auxiliary lemma packages the two-step descent that promotes
`Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` (after passing through the birational morphism
`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) to a regular morphism
`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The descent is a rational map
`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A` on the dense open of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` on which
`f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism via
Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (the sibling `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`).
This is the unique step of the proof that consumes A.4.c.

Blueprint reference: `lem:descent_through_birational_sigma` (Milne's
proof of III.6.1, p. 104, "It therefore defines a rational map
`ψ : J → A`, which (I 3.2) shows to be a regular map"). -/

/-- **Descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.**

There is a *unique* regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ`
(equivalently, `ψ` is the unique regular extension of the rational map
`Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1} : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`).

The rational map is constructed on the dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
where `f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism
on all of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` via Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (sibling
`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` in
`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c). Uniqueness is the
standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle: two regular
morphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` agreeing on a dense open are equal.

iter-178+: body invokes `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` on the
rational map produced by inverting `f^{(g)}` on its dense open of
isomorphism. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed
`sorry`. -/
theorem descentThroughBirationalSigma
    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
      symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ := by
  sorry

/-! ## §6. The Albanese universal property — headline theorem

The headline result of the chapter: for every pointed morphism
`φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with `φ(P₀) = η_A`, there
exists a unique regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
`φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}`. The proof follows Milne Proposition III.6.1
verbatim, using the four sub-lemmas above plus Milne Theorem I.3.2
(`extend_to_av`) and Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
consequence).

Blueprint reference: `thm:albanese_universal_property` (Milne,
*Abelian Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1, p. 104). -/

/-- **Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — Milne III §6
Proposition 6.1.**

For a complete nonsingular curve `C` of genus `g > 0` over an
algebraically closed field `k̄`, a marked `k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, and
every pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with
`φ(P₀) = η_A`, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that `φ = ι_{P₀} ≫ ψ`.

Proof sketch (Milne III.6.1):
1. **Symmetrisation.** `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is
   `S_g`-symmetric (`A` commutative), so factors through
   `Sym^g C` as `Sym^g φ` (`lem:symmetric_product_av_map`).
2. **Descent to a regular morphism on `J`.** The birational morphism
   `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`)
   exhibits `Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1}` as a rational map
   `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, which by Milne Theorem I.3.2 extends uniquely to
   a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`
   (`lem:descent_through_birational_sigma`).
3. **Factorisation `ψ ∘ ι_{P₀} = φ`.** Use Milne's identification of
   `ι_{P₀}` with the composite `Q ↦ Q + (g − 1) P₀` followed by
   `f^{(g)}`; together with `φ(P₀) = η_A` and the additive structure
   of `A`, this gives `ψ(ι_{P₀}(Q)) = φ(Q)` on `k̄`-points, hence
   globally by the reduced-and-separated agreement principle.
4. **Homomorphism property.** `ψ` sends `η_J = ι_{P₀}(P₀)` to
   `φ(P₀) = η_A`, so by Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
   consequence) it is a homomorphism of group schemes.
5. **Uniqueness.** Two homomorphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` with the same
   restriction to `ι_{P₀}(C)` agree on the `g`-fold sum
   `ι_{P₀}(C) + ⋯ + ι_{P₀}(C) = Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (since `f^{(g)}` is
   surjective), hence are equal.

The iter-177 file-skeleton encodes only the existence-and-uniqueness
of the factoring morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` (matching the
project's `IsAlbanese` precedent in `Jacobian.lean`); the
group-homomorphism property is implicit in the construction. The body
consumes its four sub-lemma exports plus
`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (A.4.c) and Milne Corollary I.1.2
(registered in `chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity`).

iter-178+: body assembles the five-step proof of Milne III.6.1. For
the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
theorem albanese_universal_property
    (_hg : 0 < genus C)
    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A), φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ := by
  sorry

end Pic0

end AlgebraicGeometry

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean

**Lane 7 (iter-177): File-skeleton — NEW FILE.**

## Outcome

RESOLVED — new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean` landed; the 6
blueprint-pinned declarations have non-tautological substantive type
signatures with `sorry` bodies. Import wired into `AlgebraicJacobian.lean`
alphabetically (above `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, above `Thm32RationalMapExtension`).
`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP` returns 0 errors;
file warnings = 7 (1 helper `bundle` + 6 pinned declarations).

## Pinned declarations landed

All six in the `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0` namespace, matching the
blueprint `\lean{...}` pins of
`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`:

1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi`
   (`noncomputable def`, line 240) — the regular Abel–Jacobi morphism
   `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at a marked `k̄`-point `P₀`.
   Substantive type: `C ⟶ jacobianScheme C`.
2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower`
   (`noncomputable def`, line 287) — the `g`-th symmetric power
   `Sym^g C` as a `k̄`-scheme. Substantive type: `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.
3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap`
   (`noncomputable def`, line 322) — symmetrised morphism
   `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` from input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
   Substantive type: `SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A`.
4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian`
   (`noncomputable def`, line 362) — birational morphism
   `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`.
   Substantive type: `SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C`.
5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma`
   (`theorem`, line 401) — `∃!` descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular
   `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. Substantive `∃!` statement.
6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property`
   (`theorem`, line 466) — the headline UP theorem (Milne III.6.1):
   `∃!` factoring `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` with `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ`.

Each pinned declaration unfolds to a typed `sorry`; no
`Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` / empty-content
`proof_wanted` shortcuts.

## Helper landed (1 of budget 1)

The A.3 row (identity-component `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of the Picard scheme) is
not yet split out into a dedicated chapter / .lean file, so the 6 pins
need a *file-internal placeholder* to refer to `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The single
helper carrying a `sorry` is:

- `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.bundle (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ... : Bundle C`
  (`noncomputable def`, line 183) — typed `sorry`. The `Bundle` structure
  bundles the underlying scheme together with the four abelian-variety

6. `Pic0.albanese_universal_property` — assembles the five-step
   Milne III.6.1 proof:

   (2) descent via `lem:descent_through_birational_sigma`,
   (3) factorisation `ψ ∘ ι_{P₀} = φ` via Milne's identification

       (registered in `chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity`),

This factoring matches the chapter (the sub-lemmas are stated for

No instance-binder threading was needed in this file. The 4 abelian-variety
